title: `docker run` has no output without `-i` on macOS using Podman helper
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

On macOS, with mac helper installed, `docker run` prints out nothing unless `-i` is given, which is different from Docker Desktop (and Docker on Linux).

`podman run` prints out command output normally.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `docker run --rm archlinux:latest ls`
2. Run `docker run --rm -i archlinux:latest ls`
3. Run `DOCKER_HOST=ssh://root@Podman docker run --rm archlinux:latest ls` (I added `Podman` to my ssh config)
4. Run `podman run --rm archlinux:latest ls`

**Describe the results you received:**

No output in step 1; was able to see `ls` output in step 2-4

**Describe the results you expected:**

Can see `ls` output in step 1

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.18.8
Built:        Thu Nov 10 07:43:58 2022
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.19.2
Built:        Fri Oct 21 19:09:51 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

(4.3.1 server hasn't hit CoreOS yet.)

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

```sh
# in macOS
$ docker --version
Docker version 20.10.21, build baeda1f
```

comments.body: I take it all of the docker commands were run with DOCKER_HOST pointing at the podman.sock?
@rhatdan Yes, though not directly but via the symbolic link created by mac helper from `/var/run/docker.sock` to `$HOME/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman.sock`
@mheon you might have some idea on this one?
Not really, we don't do testing against the Docker CLI. I can look into it, though.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Is this still a problem? I do not have a mac I cannot reproduce on linux.

Looks like the issue is gone now

```
$ docker --version
Docker version 23.0.5, build bc4487a

$ podman version

Version:      4.5.0

Built:        Wed Mar  1 22:22:59 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

$ docker run --rm archlinux:latest ls
```
